corrections.old_value: Partial enhancement of SERAT activity is a plausible strategy to sustain cysteine and glutathione synthesis and maintain glucose-TOR-dependent growth under sulfur-limited or sulfur-assimilation-impaired conditions; stacking with sulfur uptake/assimilation genes may be more effective than single-gene manipulation. Strong depletion causes growth defects, so complete loss-of-function should be avoided.

corrections.old_value: TOR acts as a useful downstream hub for coupling sulfur assimilation with growth. Engineering upstream sulfur metabolism together with carbon status may be more practical than direct constitutive TOR activation, because TOR overactivation may have pleiotropic effects. Context-specific activation under favorable carbon and sulfur supply is likely preferable.
